ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?

translated: And Abraham drew near and asked, "Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?"

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.
translated: The LORD saw that the wickedness of man was great on the earth, and it grieved him in his heart.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.

translated: And Seth also had a son born to him; and he called his name Enos: then men began to call upon the name of the LORD.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.

translated: And Midian’s children were Efa, Efer, Hanok, Abida, and Eldaa. All of these were the children of Keturah.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.

translated: The young man was not hesitant to do evil; he wanted to sleep with Jacob and his daughter. Then they went to Shechem with his father and all his family.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”

translated: When your enemy sits down, he will deceive your brother; and when he comes to take your place, he will push him aside and take your position.

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.

translated: Kefa, please cook for me the food that I love, let me eat it, and let me bless you before God before I die.

translated: These were the sons of Seir the Horite, who were living in the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

translated: And he took all that he had and fled; and he rose up, and crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead. Laban pursued Jacob.

translated: And Leah conceived, and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben, which means: “See, a son”: for she said, Surely the LORD has looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me.

translated: And Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and told all these things in their ears: and the people were very afraid.

translated: And Isaac asked them, "Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have driven me away from you?"

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
